Document Title: Modeling the Impact of Public Transportation and Mitigation Strategies in COVID-19 Spread in Mexico City
Authors: F. Reyes-Gómez; E. Torres-Marín; M. E. Lárraga; L. Alvarez-Icaza; R. A. Mora-Gutiérrez
Author Affiliations: Instituto de Ingeniería, Universidad Nacional Autónoma de México, Mexico City, Mexico; Instituto de Ingeniería, Universidad Nacional Autónoma de México, Mexico City, Mexico; Instituto de Ingeniería, Universidad Nacional Autónoma de México, Mexico City, Mexico; Instituto de Ingeniería, Universidad Nacional Autónoma de México, Mexico City, Mexico; Systems Department, Autonomous Metropolitan University, Azcapotzalco Campus, Mexico City, Mexico
Abstract: Understanding the spread of infectious diseases such as COVID-19 has been a persistent challenge for scientists and policymakers. While numerous studies have researched general aspects of the COVID-19 transmission dynamics, few have specifically examined its spread within public transportation systems in highly interconnected urban environments while incorporating the role played by the degree of effectiveness of time-varying mitigation strategies. This paper presents a novel approach using a discrete, probabilistic, and age-structured model that integrates the concept of compartmental models in epidemiology with cellular automata networks to investigate the role of public transportation and mitigation strategies in COVID-19 transmission in Mexico City districts. Therefore, the transmission of the disease is based on the definition of two distinct forces of infection: one associated with the use of public transport and the other with the occurrence within each district. Furthermore, the model incorporates several key time-varying parameters such as district population, commuting patterns, and contact matrices for different settings. Moreover, a stringency index was constructed based on the Oxford index in order to evaluate the impact of mitigation strategies established by the Mexican government and their compliance. Simulation results successfully reproduce COVID-19 transmission dynamics in Mexico City districts before vaccination efforts. Findings suggest that public transportation significantly contributed to the spread of the disease during the initial phase of the pandemic in Mexico City. These insights provide valuable guidance for public health strategies to mitigate infectious disease transmission in densely populated urban environments.
Funding Information: Programa de Apoyo a Proyectos de Investigación e Innovación Tecnológica (PAPIIT)-Universidad Nacional Autónoma de México (UNAM)(grant numbers:IN101922,IN104025,IT100623,IV100220);
Author Keywords: Cellular automata network;COVID-19 transmission dynamics;discrete modeling and simulation;epidemic model;human mobility;Mexico City;public transportation
IEEE Terms: COVID-19;Urban areas;Public transportation;Automata;Prevention and mitigation;Diseases;Pandemics;Indexes;Analytical models;Probabilistic logic

Document Title: A Reasoning System for Graded Modal Logic
Author Affiliations: IIMAS: Posgrado en Ciencia e lngeniería de la Computación, Universidad Nacional Autónoma de México, Mexico City, Mexico; Facultad de Ingenería: Dpto. de Computación, Universidad Nacional Autónoma de México, Mexico City, Mexico
Publication Title: 2024 12th International Conference in Software Engineering Research and Innovation (CONISOFT)
Abstract: In this paper, we propose a deduction system for the modal logic $\mathbf{K}$ with graded modalities, which is obtained by adding numerical constraints to the modal operators. Graded modalities can be seen as a generalization of traditional modal operators since they include numerical bounds in the modalities, thus enabling the formal treatment of more detailed expressions, such as “there are at least $n+1$ individuals satisfying a certain property”, where $n$ is a natural number. This kind of properties commonly arise in contexts such as concurrent systems: “if at least 3 processors are free, then proceed to compute the next task”, or epistemic logic:”if more than 2 people know $P$, then $P$ is known”. The system is based on the notion of tree-hypersequents. We show the system is correct, i.e. sound and complete.
IEEE Terms: Technological innovation;Interpolation;Program processors;Computational modeling;Syntactics;Cognition;Numerical models;Logic;Iterative methods;Formal verification
